editorialSummary.text: This historical 18-acre Japanese garden features tea rooms & the Cultural Exchange Center.

text.text:
Visited Hakone Estate and Gardens in Saratoga and it was such a peaceful and beautiful experience. The gardens are very well maintained with traditional Japanese landscaping, koi ponds, bamboo groves, and quiet walking paths.

It’s a great place to slow down, take a walk, and enjoy nature. The scenery feels very calming and there are lots of nice spots for photos without feeling crowded. The historic estate adds to the charm and makes the visit feel special.

Overall, a really serene and unique place in the Bay Area. Definitely worth visiting if you’re in the area or want a quiet escape.

Overall, a really serene and unique place in the Bay Area. Definitely worth visiting if you’re in the area or want a quiet escape.

text.text:
I recently visited Hakone Estate and Gardens and it honestly feels like stepping into a completely different world tucked away in the Saratoga hills. The gardens are incredibly peaceful, beautifully maintained, and thoughtfully designed with winding stone paths, koi ponds, bamboo groves, bridges, waterfalls, and traditional Japanese architecture throughout the property.

What impressed me most was how serene and immersive the experience felt. Every turn offers a new perspective — from quiet garden spaces to scenic overlooks surrounded by lush greenery and seasonal flowers. It’s the kind of place where you naturally slow down and appreciate the details. The koi ponds and Moon Bridge area were especially beautiful and made for amazing photos.

I also appreciated the historical aspect of the gardens. Knowing that Hakone is one of the oldest Japanese-style residential gardens in the Western Hemisphere makes the visit even more special. The cultural authenticity and craftsmanship really stand out.

The grounds are well maintained, peaceful, and surprisingly expansive with multiple levels and hidden corners to explore. It never felt overcrowded, and the atmosphere was calm and relaxing from start to finish. This is an excellent place for a quiet walk, photography, reflection, or simply escaping the busyness of Silicon Valley for a couple of hours.

Parking was convenient and the entire property felt very well cared for. If you enjoy Japanese gardens, nature, or scenic hidden gems in the Bay Area, Hakone is absolutely worth visiting at least once — and probably many times more during different seasons.

text.text:
We recently had the pleasure of providing photobooth services for a wedding at Hakone Estate and Gardens, and the venue was absolutely stunning. The beautiful Japanese gardens, serene atmosphere, and meticulously maintained grounds created a breathtaking backdrop for both the ceremony and guest photos.

The staff was professional, accommodating, and easy to work with throughout the event, making setup and coordination seamless. Every corner of the property offers incredible photo opportunities, and guests couldn’t stop admiring the scenery. Hakone Estate and Gardens is truly a unique and unforgettable venue for weddings and special events. We look forward to returning for future celebrations!

— 3Klicks Photobooth

text.text: Came here for the night time experience and got to experience the gardens in both light (before sunset) and the dark with the lights turned on. The kids had fun exploring the grounds and enjoyed feeding the fish with the food from the gift shop. Note that not all the trails are open at night but that worked out for us because my husband can't get to the upper areas in his wheelchair. He was still able to enjoy the main area with the koi pond and the wisteria.

text.text: Sort of hidden within the residential areas of Saratoga (off Big Basin), the gardens, water falls and koi pond are an excellent way to spend a couple hours on a nice sunny day. Parking was free and easy to find a spot. There is a little area near the front gate with picnic tables (only a couple). The estate house has tea service for purchase and there is a little gift shop where you purchase tickets onsite. Entry tickets can be purchased ahead of time online, although there is no specific hour that you need to be there during the day. Set in the hills there is a walking path and ADA level path as well. It really is beautifully maintained. It's outstanding that this is/was a private estate long ago and has been here since 1915.
